decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). Since the spreader conductivity is already at its maximum bound (500.0), further material improvement is impossible. Following the optimization history suggestion to try a different variable category, I will increase the heat spreader width to enhance lateral heat spreading area, which is the next most effective lever for reducing peak temperature.

changes.reason: Increasing the spreader width maximizes the lateral area available for heat dissipation towards the cooled boundaries, directly addressing the high chip temperature now that conductivity tuning has stalled.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature due to improved lateral heat spreading.
Flatter temperature gradient across the spreader layer.
risk_notes: The new width (1.0) approaches the design domain limit; ensure no geometric overlap violations occur with other components or boundaries.
Diminishing returns may occur if the added width is too far from the heat source to effectively conduct heat to the sinks.
